The thing here is that both Goliath and Tulu want Aliemus as well, so no, it's not a waste placing 2 Aliemus charging awakeners. Moreover, Thais's kit is much more than only that. That said, Goliath is also fine to place in there. I would say that Goliath > Tulu, but that's arguable.
You can just place both and try to play with Thais being the only Aliemus charging awakener.
we talking about gmurphy team? i thought order of importance for teammates was myriam > tulu > goliath/murphy/other (just heard about thais here today)
I'm not too experienced with GMurphy teams, I just know the ideas and reasons behind those.
tulu gets stronger the less invested you are
you'll see a lot of high end/endgame players recommending against tulu
because his buffs are flat values so the value of them diminishes when you have level 80s/90s and high stats
True you like to use any other possible awakener instead of tulu
But I think the raw tent dmg and str helps early
he's also one of the strongest sources of teamwide crit for tentacle teams but that becomes less of an issue once you have high rolled covs and good wheel choices

Order is a subject to change since depending on the player, you might want to choose other options. Like placing Murphy at the end of the list might not be a wise decision since within Arc 1 and especially newcomers gameplay players might experience a lot of problems with surviving due to not knowing how to optimise and play. Furthermore, Murphy's exalt is insanely good and her 3 cost helps with reducing Sacrifice stacks.
It's fine to use her even within Arc 2 if you feel like it
Doesnt "gained crit" have low value for tent crit than "base crit"?

but the thing is like, for any level of team that isn't hyper endgame high level, tulu buffs are very large and start the scaling off very strongly, especially as goliath is a % based buff that gets stronger the higher your numbers are and non-oe gmurphy isn't nearly as arithmetica-reliant due to not spawning 3 waltzes
What happens to Murphy in arc 2?
not sure what exactly you mean, there's no such thing as gained crit vs base crit
crit is crit, tentacle crit is simply the average value of your team's crit
tulu crit buffs are all teamwide so they apply to tentacles in full
Crit gained from explo and before starting the explo are calculated differently for tent crit, no?
huh, no
why would they be
<:dead:1356082627509092372> I thought someone sent a ss of that here in this channel
~~tentacle crit is simply the average of everyone's crit~~ average x2 for implicit crit via wheels/covs/char stats
add everyone's crit rates together and divide by 4
you might be confusing individual crit with team crit
because built-in crit from sources such as wheel stats and covenants only apply to the character using them
for example if april gives 35% critrate, it only gives it to the person with april equipped
and because tentacle crit is the average crit value across your whole team, one guy with +3*% critrate and 3 people with +0% critrate means april is only giving 1/4th of 35% to tentacle crit
conversely teamwide crit buffs such as tulu's buffs give 15% critrate to everyone on the team, so 1/4th of 15% contributing 4 times is 15% in full
That one SR wheel with 25% crit
Also the cursed relic, hook
Ic ic

anyway the general tl;dr is that if you're in this channel asking questions about gmurphy team comps, you are likely not at a point where tulu values are weak for your team so you should be using tulu
yes
those all apply in full because they're teamwide buffs, everyone on the team gets +50% critrate from hook so 50% x4 divided by 4 is still 50%
Yeah that's why I mentioned them
So I wasn't being schizo <:takenotes:1357186646294138890>
oh
you're right
oops i was wrong, never realized that
so tent crit is actually double the value of the average and not the average for inherent crit values
which doesn't really change much since it's not particularly relevant since it's only referring to wheel/cov stats but that's neat to know
Gol has good base crit
yeah he's one of the chars with crit growth
Also thanks<:Cheerup:1400771555939844149>
Lens do you have the tl
Or should I ai it
initial tentacle critrate/critdmg is 50% of the total on entering battle (i.e., implicit out-of-combat crit stats)
inbattle tentacle critrate/critdmg increases is 25% of the total (i.e., averaged across the team)
basically it's just saying that critrate/critdmg mainstats from wheels, covenants, and char growth is twice as effective
Cov set effect too ig?
implicit yes, set effects like april or crimson no
because those are inbattle buffs
but 3pc set effects count
basically if it's on the character statsheet it counts as out of combat

Clem is really good with kath and just really good in general. I really like the "ultra kath" version of kath teams and clem is a really important part of that because her exalt debuff can either help you stall out a boss by stacking with weakness during early turns or burst a boss in later turns by stacking with vuln. The strength gain and skey from clem is also nice.

But its hard to say that you should pick her up over e3 kath because kath really doesn't exist as a unit before e3. If you already have at least one dps you are happy with then I think picking up clem and waiting until next month to e3 kath would be reasonable.

hello!

if I understand correctly, the waves, mask, teeth and star all signify different realm, escentially different types, each type brings with them their type mechanic into a team, so if you bring a wave character you can make use of tentacles, if you bring a teeth character like Faint you can create embryos to generate energy for your ultimate  so on and so forth…

if you just want to know what teams to run, pull on Thais and Murphy for at least one copy->Two additional copies of Thais so she can pop off->ideally three more copies of Murphy but if you can’t get three its fine 

in the Realm Study you were likely prompted to select a realm, pick blue/aequor/wave and get a few characters from there and then just go around grabbing as you see fit

this is a lot of info to take in so please tell me if I lose you somewhere <:takenotes:1357186646294138890>

Defensive Options (Shield, Counter, Weakness, Temporary Strength Down, Poison, Duplication) 

Offensive Options (Permanent Strength, Temporary Strength, Duplication, Vulnerability, Counter, Poison)

Utility Options (Duplication, Gain Arithmatecia, Permanent, Card Draw, Permanent Strength, Poison, Vulnerability, Weakness, Temporary Strength Down)

Omg completely miss read sorry
I believe corrosion is removed based on damage dealt and it doesn't have any specific affinity for single vs multi hit attacks. You just want something that hits hard.
thank you, i looked up a tierlist but wasnt sure what the pool is there,
Thanks!
As far as I'm aware, it doesn't matter. You just need to have a sub-dps to proc his corrosion. Any active/tentacle dmg procs it fully, while non-active dmg procs half of it.
i mean lol standard rly means nth to me as a new player who never heard about this game

The tierlists are bad since some "F" tier chars are used in some specific comps and that's why they are placed lower. I wouldn't trust them completely.
In the beginning don’t worry too much about it, the early chapters are super chill! Read the story and find which path you like the best and built towards is as you get an insane amount of pull!
Plus some people making tierlists are biased.
